utility_notes: preserve hadron-background class h coverage; h is the local minority class but is scientifically important for background rejection
Alpha is the strongest single h-vs-g utility feature in local checks, with Length and Width also highly informative
M3Long and Asym carry useful signed moment/asymmetry signal and should not be treated as noise
Conc and Conc1 have weak direct target association but are central to realistic image-shape dependency through Size
target class must never appear in theta even though it is the label

pair_priors: two concentration ratios are near-deterministically coupled and must remain coherent
total image size and concentration ratio are strongly inversely related
highest-pixel concentration depends strongly on total image size
major and minor ellipse axes jointly define shower-image geometry
image width scales with total light-yield structure
major-axis length scales with image size and event morphology
ellipse length and distance from camera origin are moderately dependent
major-axis extent and longitudinal asymmetry should stay physically plausible together
signed asymmetry and third moment along the major axis describe related longitudinal structure
concentration ratio shifts with event distance from origin
single-pixel concentration and event distance should remain coherent
orientation angle and distance jointly affect image placement relative to the source direction
minor-axis width and concentration ratio are strongly inversely related
minor-axis width and highest-pixel concentration should not drift independently
longitudinal moment and orientation angle both contribute to class-discriminating morphology

shape: all feature columns are continuous; synthetic data can over-smooth high-resolution event signatures
Length, Width, Size, and Dist are right-tailed, so large event/image-scale values should not be flattened
Alpha is bounded in [0, 90] and highly nonuniform; it should not be generated outside bounds or washed toward the center
Conc and Conc1 are bounded ratios with strong coupling; unrealistic ratio values can break image physics
Asym, M3Long, and M3Trans are signed and heavy-tailed; clipping negative values would distort morphology
Width can be exactly zero in local train data; do not force all width values strictly positive
trend: Conc, Conc1, and Size have the strongest dependency structure and should not be sampled independently
Length, Width, and Size jointly define ellipse/image scale and must remain coherent
Asym and M3Long encode related signed longitudinal structure
Alpha and Dist jointly describe image orientation/placement relative to the camera origin
weak direct utility variables such as Conc and Conc1 still matter because they anchor realistic dependency structure
privacy: configured privacy columns are Length, Width, Size, Alpha, and Dist
there is no direct human PII, but DCR/privacy risk is still relevant because continuous event descriptors can be near-unique
combining ellipse geometry, orientation, and signed moment fields can create narrow event-level fingerprints
col_ps should be broad for the 10-feature schema but must not include all feature columns
privacy wording should emphasize memorization and nearest-neighbor disclosure rather than demographic privacy
utility: dropping Alpha will substantially weaken h-vs-g classification utility
h is the local minority class and background class; synthetic data should preserve background-like high-Alpha and large-axis patterns
using only Conc or Conc1 as utility focus is risky because their direct target association is weak despite strong dependency value
signed morphology features such as M3Long and Asym should remain available for utility even though they have broad tails
col_u should usually be Alpha, Length, Width, M3Long, or Asym; never use the target column

seed_theta_examples.reason: preserves high-risk marginal shapes for orientation, ellipse scale, image size, and distance; col_2ds includes ratio anchors but keeps a geometry-first combination
concentrates on Conc/Conc1/Size, Length/Width, and signed longitudinal structure; col_ps omits Alpha/Dist so this is not the same event-geometry privacy scope
broad DCR/privacy scope covers configured event-geometry columns plus high-resolution ratio and signed-moment descriptors without using all features
uses the strongest h-vs-g utility feature while retaining ellipse geometry, signed morphology, and configured privacy anchors
